messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online course: Introduction to Meta-Analysis
messages.subject_clean: Online course: Introduction to Meta-Analysis
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is offering a new 3-day workshop on research synthesis: Introduction to Meta-Analysis <https://instats.org/seminar/introduction-to-meta-analysis-2>, running Nov 20 - 22 by the CASC group at UCL. This comprehensive 3-day program is designed to equip participants with the knowledge and skills necessary to conduct and interpret meta-analyses, allowing researchers to identify patterns, contradictions, and other relationships that might be missed in individual studies. This workshop is particularly valuable for those seeking to implement the method in or R and Stata, with a separate half-day seminar on using each.

Sign up today to secure your spot in this seminar, and please feel free to tell your friends and colleagues.


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org <http://instats.org>
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] online course: Introduction to the Analysis of Longitudinal Data in Biology using R
messages.subject_clean: online course: Introduction to the Analysis of Longitudinal Data in Biology using R
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
Are you ready to tackle the unique challenges of analyzing longitudinal data in biology? Join us from November 25-28 for an immersive online course, Introduction to the Analysis of Longitudinal Data in Biology using R.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/longitudinal-data-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/longitudinal-data-in-r/ )
 
Over four days, you?ll explore:
 Statistical & machine-learning perspectives on time-series and spatial data
Practical applications in epidemiology, gene expression, and survival analysis
Hands-on R sessions, collaborative exercises, and a wrap-up quiz!
This course is perfect for biologists, students, and researchers eager to master the tools for visualizing, modeling, and interpreting longitudinal data.
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Looking to commission help for analysis of radio telemetry spatial data
messages.subject_clean: Looking to commission help for analysis of radio telemetry spatial data
messages.body_plain:
Hi there,

We are looking to recruit someone to help analyse spatial data collected using radio tags placed in wild fish. We are looking to estimate home range sizes for around 10 individuals and explore habitat associations in the study lake (wetland, forest edge, etc.).

If you are interested and available, please email hello at ethicalseafoodresearch.com for more information.

Many thanks,

Wasseem


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Looking to commission help for analysis of radio telemetry spatial data
messages.subject_clean: Looking to commission help for analysis of radio telemetry spatial data
messages.body_plain:
Hi Wasseem

In case it's helpful, Instats (instats.org) offers a marketplace for this
type of research consulting. Just go to 'Collaborators' and filter by
consultants:

https://instats.org/?v=&exptype=consult&explistsize=81&expertliststart=1

You can search for specific skillsets or disciplines. Two specific expert
profiles come to mind here:

https://instats.org/expert/geospatial-training-solutions-2
https://instats.org/expert/nick-bearman

Anyone with a PhD or equivalent can freely offer their services through the
platform, which has integrated billing/invoicing and global payment
processing services in the back-end. If anyone with a PhD or equivalent
would like to have a profile on the platform, please get in touch with me
and I'm happy to help. Instats also has a new tutorial and demonstration of
our platform's partner features, which make creating new seminars and
workshops extremely easy with our AI-enabled content builder. Feel free to
check out the on-demand streaming video here
<https://instats.org/seminar/run-your-seminars-workshops-and-confer3> and
get in touch if you'd like to join the platform. We're all academics, so
the platform is free to use and we never try to own anyone's IP or sell
their data.

Best wishes

Mike


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
*instats.org* <http://instats.org>

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Looking to commission help for analysis of radio telemetry spatial data
messages.subject_clean: Looking to commission help for analysis of radio telemetry spatial data
messages.body_plain:
Thanks Oli and Mike!

I never received your reply sent to the mailing list (I guess it will come as part of the daily digest) so thanks to Oli for flagging it as well.

I am actually amazed at the number of replies we received today, I wish I had known about this list earlier!

I will review your email properly tomorrow and be in touch.

Many thanks again to both!

Wasseem
________________________________

I work with Michael and Instats would be a great place to search. I should have mentioned them when you emailed me, apologies!

Best,

Oliver

PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Learn Statistics in R for Biology & Ecology – Join Us Jan 7-10, 2025!
messages.subject_clean: Learn Statistics in R for Biology & Ecology – Join Us Jan 7-10, 2025!
messages.body_plain:
Hi all,
Start the New Year by enhancing your analytical skills! Join our Introduction to Statistics in R for Biologists and Ecologists course, held online from January 7-10, 2025.
This beginner-friendly course will guide you through R and RStudio to perform essential statistical analyses in biology and ecology. You?ll learn:
Data insights with tidyverse and ggplot2
Descriptive statistics for data summaries
Inferential statistics to make data-driven decisions
Linear and generalized linear models for complex analyses
Classes run 2-8 pm Berlin time each day and include interactive lectures and hands-on exercises. No prior experience in R or statistics required!
For full details and registration, visit: https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course13/
Best regards,
Carlo


--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Units and mvabund
messages.subject_clean: Units and mvabund
messages.body_plain:
Dear list: My graduate student and I have noticed that our mvabund results are sensitive to the units of our data (i.e. grams or milligrams ? in other words, a thousandfold difference). Is this because of inflation of differences between zeros and non-zeros? Any insight would be greatly appreciated. Yours, Jon.

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online Course: Python for R Users - last chance
messages.subject_clean: Online Course: Python for R Users - last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance to sign up for our new 1-day seminar Python for R Users <https://instats.org/seminar/python-for-r-users2>, running Nov 13 with professor Rebecca Barter from the Division of Epidemiology at the University of Utah School of Medicine. For researchers who are familiar with R, especially within the tidyverse ecosystem, transitioning to Python offers a strategic advantage for academic research. Python's versatility and broad applicability across a huge range of scientific fields make it a crucial tool for complex data analysis and model building. To facilitate this, the seminar is designed to help PhD students, academics, and professional researchers leverage their existing R knowledge to quickly gain proficiency in Python, focusing on translating concepts and techniques familiar in R to the Python environment. Participants will gain a robust understanding of Python's libraries and data structures, enabling them to integrate Python into their research workflows efficiently and expand their analytical capabilities, including with Python's substantial toolkits for machine learning and deep learning such as Scikit-learn and Keras (for machine learning and deep learning).

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/python-for-r-users2> and don't miss out on this unique opportunity to easily transition from R to Python!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org <http://instats.org>
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Units and mvabund
messages.subject_clean: Units and mvabund
messages.body_plain:
Dear Jon,

Which distribution was used? Normal (Gaussian) distribution should be 
resistant to change units. Poisson and negative binomial are sensitive. 
I'm not sure about tweedie.

Best wishes,

Zoltan

Is this because of inflation of differences between zeros and non-zeros? Any insight would be greatly appreciated. Yours, Jon.

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] [EXTERNAL] Re:  Units and mvabund
messages.subject_clean: [EXTERNAL] Re:  Units and mvabund
messages.body_plain:
Dear Zoltan,

Many thanks. My student is using the negative binomial. So a Gaussian distribution is recommended for biomass data?

Yours, Jon.

From: R-sig-ecology <r-sig-ecology-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Zolt?n Botta-Duk?t <botta-dukat.zoltan at ecolres.hu>
Date: Friday, November 8, 2024 at 2:29 AM
To: r-sig-ecology at r-project.org <r-sig-ecology at r-project.org>
Subject: [EXTERNAL] Re: [R-sig-eco] Units and mvabund
[You don't often get email from botta-dukat.zoltan at ecolres.hu. Learn why this is important at https://aka.ms/LearnAboutSenderIdentification ]

Dear Jon,

Which distribution was used? Normal (Gaussian) distribution should be
resistant to change units. Poisson and negative binomial are sensitive.
I'm not sure about tweedie.

Best wishes,

Zoltan

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Master Evidence Synthesis with Meta-analysis in R (Online Course - Feb 10-14, 2025)
messages.subject_clean: Master Evidence Synthesis with Meta-analysis in R (Online Course - Feb 10-14, 2025)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
registrations are now open for the online course on Meta-analysis in R.
 
This online course (Feb 10-14, 2025) equips you with the skills to conduct robust evidence synthesis in ecology and evolution using meta-analysis in R.
 
It includes a mix of lectures and hands-on exercises using real meta-analytic datasets. The emphasis throughout the course is on the application of the various methods and the interpretation of the results using the free software R and the R packages ?metafor? (Viechtbauer 2010) and ?orchaRd? (Nakagawa et al. 2023). The course will follow the principles of open science, with a strong focus on the importance of adhering to preferred reporting items for systematic reviews and meta-analyses in ecology and evolutionary biology (PRISMA EcoEvo; O?Dea et al. 2021). Throughout, we will consider examples of how to interpret results and present them using tables and data visualization, and for each step, we will provide literature and practical resources (e.g., R scripts).
 
For more information, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/metain-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/metain-r/ )
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846




	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Single Cell Data Analysis - Livestream Seminar
messages.subject_clean: Single Cell Data Analysis - Livestream Seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance for the 1-day seminar on Single Cell Data Analysis <https://instats.org/seminar/single-cell-data-analysis2> in R and Python, running Nov 26 with professor Nikolay Oskolkov. Single-cellanalysis provides unprecedented insights into cellular heterogeneity, enabling researchers to explore complex biological systems at the most granular scale. With applications in cancer research, developmental biology, and immunology, among others, mastering the techniques of single-celldataanalysis is essential for researchers aiming to push the boundaries of modern science. This one-day workshop offers an in-depth exploration of the computational tools and methodologies essential for high-level single-celldata interpretation.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/single-cell-data-analysis2> to secure your spot in this unique seminar.


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org <http://instats.org>
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online Course: Computational Network Science - Last Chance
messages.subject_clean: Online Course: Computational Network Science - Last Chance
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance to sign up for our 5-day livestreaming seminar on Computational Network Science Using Python <https://instats.org/seminar/computational-network-science-using-pyth2>, running November 18 - 22 with professor Moses Boudourides. Networkscience has become a crucial tool in understanding complex systems across multiple fields. The analysis of networks enables researchers to uncover hidden patterns, decipher sophisticated structures, and understand dynamic processes in both natural and social phenomena. This workshop can be highly beneficial for the veterinary epidemiological community by offering tools to analyze complex networks of disease transmission, animal movement, and epidemiological patterns. Understanding these networks can enhance the ability to predict outbreaks, trace infection pathways, and improve decision-making in managing animal health and zoonotic diseases. The sessions will empower participants by equipping them with the skills necessary to construct, manipulate, and analyze networks using state-of-the-art methodologies and tools. This workshop is tailored for PhD students, academics, and professional researchers across a variety of disciplines, and professor Boudourides?s extensive experience and expertise in computationalnetworkscience ensures that attendees will receive not only excellent instruction but also hands-on experience.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/computational-network-science-using-pyth2> to secure your spot in this unique event - and please feel free to tell your colleagues.


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org <http://instats.org>
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:
Hi everyone

Last chance to sign up for our 5-day livestreaming seminar on Computational Network Science Using Python <https://instats.org/seminar/computational-network-science-using-pyth2>, running N

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Applied Regression with Continuous and Categorical Data - Livestream Seminar
messages.subject_clean: Applied Regression with Continuous and Categorical Data - Livestream Seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance to sign up for our 4-day seminar Applied Regression with Continuous and Categorical Data <https://instats.org/seminar/applied-regression-with-continuous-and-c2> using R and Stata, running Nov 19 - 22 by the Department of Epidemiology and Public Health at UCL. This comprehensive 4-day workshop equips researchers with advanced skills in both linear and Poisson regression analysis using real-world health data. This course provides a unique opportunity to learn and apply regression techniques using real-world data across a wide variety of cases that researchers experience on a regular basis -- an essential skill set for researchers aiming to publish in high-impact journals. By the end of the course, participants will be able to confidently analyze complex datasets and interpret the results in order to draw meaningful conclusions that advance their respective fields. Through hands-on exercises, participants will enhance their data preparation, model building, and interpretation capabilities.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/applied-regression-with-continuous-and-c2> to secure your spot in this unique event - and please feel free to tell your colleagues.


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org <http://instats.org>

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Introduction to GLLVM and multivariate GLMM
messages.subject_clean: Introduction to GLLVM and multivariate GLMM
messages.body_plain:
We would like to announce the following online statistics course:

Online course: Introduction to GLLVM and multivariate GLMM
When: *2 - 5 December, 09.00-16.00 UK*?time OR *9 - 12 December, 
15.00-21.00 UK time (10.00-16.00 EST) *
Website: https://www.highstat.com/


The central theme of this course is the analysis of multiple correlated 
response (or dependent) variables using GLMs and GLMMs. Rather
than applying multiple univariate GLMs or GLMMs, we will focus on 
multivariate GLMMs, particularly generalised linear latent variable
models (GLLVMs), for the simultaneous analysis of all variables.

During the course, we cover a large number of exercises with examples 
such as trait variables from turtle hatchlings from multiple clutches,
biomass data from fish species sampled at multiple sites, count data 
from 250 freshwater benthic species sampled at 200 sites, abundances
of multiple parasite species on fish, counts of 60 different debris 
types in water samples, abundances of multiple spider species in traps, 
multiple
morphometric variables sampled from honeybees, and absence/presence of 
diet variables from faecal samples of brown bears.

In all these examples, we can analyse each variable with a univariate 
GLM(M). Although these analyses are relatively simple, there are also
some problems:

  * Extra Work: Individual analyses are computationally less efficient
    and require separate validation, interpretation, and reporting.
  * Lack of Multivariate Relationships: Analysing the variables
    individually neglects the interconnected relationships and
    interactions between them.
  * No Shared Variation: Univariate models might overlook consistent
    residual patterns across species, while multivariate models can
    capture shared variations due to common environmental factors.
  * Multiple Testing: Conducting separate analyses increases the risk of
    Type I errors, especially when the response variables are highly
    correlated.
  * Loss of Community-Level Insights: Analysing species separately
    misses out on a comprehensive, community-level viewpoint and can
    lead to inconsistent conclusions.


Kind regards,

Alain

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject_clean: Online Course: Introduction to Web Apps with R Shiny

Hi everyone

Last chance to sign up for our 2-day seminar Introduction to Web App Development with R Shiny <https://instats.org/seminar/introduction-to-web-app-development-with2>, running Nov 20 - 21. This workshop provides an extensive introduction to developing interactive web applications using R Shiny, enabling participants to create, deploy, and optimize data-driven web tools. Ideal for PhD students, academics, and professional researchers, the course covers fundamental and advanced concepts tailored to enhance research presentation and real-time analytical communication across various scientific domains. From big to small data cases, the ability to build and deploy web applications using R Shiny will significantly improve participants ability to convincingly communicate their research findings to a broad audience -- including journal and grant reviewers.

Sign up <https://instats.org/seminar/introduction-to-web-app-development-with2> today to secure your spot in this unique event, and please feel free to tell your colleagues and students.


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online Course: Multivariate Data Analysis with R and vegan
messages.subject_clean: Online Course: Multivariate Data Analysis with R and vegan
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are happy to announce the upcoming Physalia online course: "Multivariate Data Analysis with R and vegan", taking place from 10-13 February 2025. 

 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/vegan/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/vegan/ ) 
 
What you?ll learn:
Key multivariate analysis techniques, including constrained and unconstrained ordination (CCA, RDA, PERMANOVA, etc.).
Effective use of the vegan R package for ordination, diversity analysis, and ecological simulations.
Hands-on application of restricted permutation tests for experimental design analysis.
Practical R programming skills for analysing and visualizing multivariate data.
Format:
Online, fostering international participation.
Sessions from 14:00 to 20:00 Berlin time, blending lectures, practical exercises, and interactive Q&A.
Who should join:
This course is ideal for those with a basic understanding of RStudio and R programming who wish to advance their knowledge of multivariate data analysis.
 
Why join?
Gain practical expertise to confidently analyze complex datasets and apply cutting-edge statistical methods using R.
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities Using R With The VEGAN package (VGNR07)
messages.subject_clean: Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities Using R With The VEGAN package (VGNR07)
messages.body_plain:
ONLINE COURSE ? Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities Using R
With The VEGAN package (VGNR07)

https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr07/
<https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr06/>

*31st - March - 4th April 2025*

Please feel free to share!

We encourage attendees to bring their own data, you will
receive opportunities to discuss your data with the instructor throughout
the course, if you would like guideline on how to organize your data
prior to the course please ask oliverhoooker at prstatistica.com

This course is suitable for researchers (PhD and MSc students, post-docs,
primary investigators) and environmental professionals who are interested
in implementing best practices and state-of-the-art methods for modelling
species? distributions or ecological niches, with applications to
biogeography, spatial ecology, biodiversity conservation and related
disciplines.

This 5-day course will cover R concepts, methods, and tools that can be
used to analyze community ecology data. The course will review data
processing techniques relevant to multivariate data sets. We will cover
diversity indices, distance measures and distance-based multivariate
methods, clustering, classification and ordination techniques using the R
package VEGAN. We will use real-world empirical data sets to motivate
analyses, such as describing patterns along gradients of environ-mental or
anthropogenic disturbances, and quantifying the effects of continuous and
discrete predictors. We will emphasise visualisation and reproducible
workflows as well as good programming practices. The modules will consist
of introductory lectures, guided computer coding, and participant
exercises. The course is intended for intermediate users of R who are
interested in community ecology, particularly in the areas of terrestrial
and wetland ecology, microbial ecology, and natural resource management.
You are strongly encouraged to use your own data sets (they should be clean
and already structured, see the document: ?recommendation if you
participate with your data?.

*Classes will run from 08:00 ? 13:00 for the morning lecture and 14:00 ?
16:00 for the practical (UK time) with an evening time session tbc for US,
Canada etc. attendees. The course will be recorded and made available each
day and will remain available for 28 days after the course for you to
revisit any lectures.*

DAY 1
? Module 1: Introduction to community data analysis, basics of programming
in R
? Module 2: Diversity analysis, species-abundance distributions

DAY 2
? Module 3: Distance and transformation measures
? Module 4: Clustering and classification analysis

DAY 3
? Module 5: Unconstrained ordinations: Principal Component Analysis
? Module 6: Other unconstrained ordinations

DAY 4
? Module 7: Constrained ordinations: RDA and other canonical analysis
? Module 8: Statistical tests for multivariate data and variation
partitioning

DAY 5
? Module 9: Overview of Spatial analysis, and recent Hierarchical Modeling
of Species Communities (HMSC) methods
? Modules 10: Special topics and discussion, analyzing participants? data.


Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions


-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject_clean: Online course -  Longitudinal Data Analysis in R (December 16-19)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

there are still a few seats available for the online course on Longitudinal Data Analysis in R.

When: December 16th-19th


Course website: https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/longitudinal-data-in-r/ 


This course covers methods and challenges in analyzing longitudinal data. It explores techniques for visualizing, analyzing, and modeling data collected over time or space. We will start by introducing the general concepts and approaches to deal with longitudinal data; we will then explore applications to specific scientific domains where longitudinal data are common: forecasting, epidemiology, gene expression.

For the full list of our courses and workshops, please visit: https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Academic Paper Writing Course (8-10 Jan 2025)
messages.subject_clean: Academic Paper Writing Course (8-10 Jan 2025)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
Are you ready to transform your research into a compelling scientific publication? Join us for our "Academic Paper Writing: Producing a First Draft of Your Paper" course, held online from 8?10 January 2025, designed to help doctoral students in the natural and life sciences craft a polished manuscript.
 
Why attend?
Comprehensive Guidance: Learn the structure of scientific publications and develop a first draft using proven techniques.
Interactive Learning: Short lectures, practical exercises, and "speed-writing sprints" tailored to your work.
Peer Feedback: Engage in collaborative review sessions to refine your manuscript.
Expert Support: Submit your draft within two weeks for personalized feedback from the instructor.
 
 
Schedule:
 Daily sessions run from 2?7 PM (Berlin time), including lectures, exercises, and group discussions.
 
For details and registration: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/paper-writing/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/paper-writing/ )
 
 
Best regards,
 Carlo

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Course: Single Cell Data Analysis - Last chance
messages.subject_clean: Course: Single Cell Data Analysis - Last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance for the 1-day seminar on Single Cell Data Analysis in R and Python <https://instats.org/seminar/single-cell-data-analysis2>, running Nov 26 with professor Nikolay Oskolkov. Single-cell analysis provides unprecedented insights into cellular heterogeneity, enabling researchers to explore complex biological systems at the most granular scale. With applications in cancer research, developmental biology, and immunology, among others, mastering the techniques of single-cell data analysis is essential for researchers aiming to push the boundaries of modern science. This one-day workshop offers an in-depth exploration of the computational tools and methodologies essential for high-level single-cell data interpretation.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/single-cell-data-analysis2> to secure your spot in this unique seminar! 


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org <http://instats.org>

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online course: GIS in R (13 -17 January 2025 )
messages.subject_clean: Online course: GIS in R (13 -17 January 2025 )
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re excited to announce our upcoming online course: "Mapping and Spatial Operations in R: Using R as a GIS", scheduled from 13-17 January 2025.
 
This course offers a comprehensive introduction to using R as a Geographic Information System (GIS) for automating and reproducing spatial data analysis. It combines theoretical lessons with practical hands-on sessions, providing participants with the skills to map and analyze environmental and biodiversity data efficiently.
Who Should Attend?
This course is ideal for researchers and practitioners eager to script reproducible and automatable spatial analysis workflows in R. A basic familiarity with R is recommended, but all scripts will be provided and explained in detail.
Learning Outcomes
By the end of this course, you will be able to:
Understand GIS basics, mapping, and cartographic projections.
Import spatial data into R and create detailed maps.
Perform diverse spatial operations and export results.
 
Seats are limited! Visit our website for more details and registration: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gis-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gis-in-r/ )
 
Best regards,
Carlo



--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Bayesian Data Analysis in R – Online Course (3-7 March 2025)
messages.subject_clean: Bayesian Data Analysis in R – Online Course (3-7 March 2025)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re excited to inform you that registrations are now open for our Bayesian Data Analysis in R course, running online from 3rd to 7th March 2025. This course offers both beginners and those with some prior knowledge of Bayesian methods a chance to gain practical, hands-on experience with Bayesian regression models.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course46/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course46/ )
 
This course will provide a solid foundation in Bayesian data analysis using practical examples and hands-on exercises. You?ll learn how to apply Bayesian methods to regression models, assess model fit, and interpret the results, while gaining confidence in using Bayesian inference for real-world applications.
 
By the end of the course, participants will:
Understand the basics of Bayesian inference.
Fit various regression models and assess model fit.
Select priors using predictive checks and validate models.
Communicate results effectively with visualizations.
Course Outline
Monday: Introduction, basics of Bayesian analysis, generative-process models
 Tuesday: MCMC methods, Bayesian parameter inference
 Wednesday: Model checking (prior & posterior checks), generalized linear models
 Thursday: Model comparison (Bayes Factors), multi-level modeling
 Friday: Best practices, final discussions and Q&A
Prerequisites
Basic knowledge of R (tidyverse package will be used).
Previous experience in Bayesian analysis is helpful but not required.
Why Join?
This course will equip you with practical skills for applying Bayesian analysis to your own data. Whether you're just starting out or looking to deepen your knowledge, this course provides the confidence to tackle Bayesian data analysis.
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846
